name: Coast Guard Sector Guam
tagline: The Coast Guard's hub in the western Pacific, guarding Guam and the Mariana Islands.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Guam (Guam, western Pacific). Region flagged OCONUS. Guam is a non-foreign OCONUS U.S. TERRITORY: NOT in the BAH file -> uses OHA (like NB Guam/Andersen already in app). So OHA/territory template (OHA housing, DoDEA Pacific Guam schools, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy). Caribbean-equivalent in the Pacific: SAR + law enforcement across the Marianas. Medical: CG clinic + US Naval Hospital Guam + TRICARE. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, DoDEA Pacific, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Role: Coast Guard hub for the western Pacific
data.Mission: Search and rescue, law enforcement, and port security across Guam and the Marianas
data.Status: Guam is a U.S. territory; treated as an overseas tour (OHA, not BAH)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Guam is the service's hub in the western Pacific, running search and rescue, law enforcement, and port security across Guam and the Mariana Islands, a vast and strategically important area.

Guam is a U.S. territory, so it's American soil, but a tour here is treated as a non-foreign overseas assignment for pay and logistics: you use the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) rather than BAH, in a tropical island setting with a distinct CHamoru culture.

sections.title: An Island Territory Tour: Tropical, Strategic, and Remote

data.subtitle: Guam is a beautiful tropical island, but remote, and everything ships in. The main choice is government/military housing versus renting on the island with OHA.
intro: Because Guam is a U.S. territory treated as an overseas tour, you'll use the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA), not the stateside BAH, or take government housing. Guam has a large military community (Navy and Air Force), so military services are well established.
Off base, families rent around the island; island realities like shipping a vehicle, higher prices, and typhoon season are part of the deal.

recommendations.advice: Government/military housing keeps you close to services on a remote island; coordinate early.
Renting around the island with OHA offers more local immersion.
The housing office assigns government housing, approves economy rentals, and helps with OHA, so start there.
Higher prices, shipping times, and typhoon season are part of a Guam tour; budget and prepare.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access follows standard installation rules. Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to be sponsored and registered through the Visitor Control Center.
Requirements can change with the security environment, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (Government & OHA)
data: Guam has government/military family housing (the island hosts a large Navy and Air Force community), and many families rent on the economy with the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns government housing, approves economy rentals, and helps with OHA. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of eligible personnel attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools on Guam, serving the island's large military community from elementary through high school.
Confirm current schools, boundaries, and registration through DoDEA Pacific and the command, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Care: On-base clinic care plus access to U.S. Naval Hospital Guam; complex care may require off-island referral

data.Commissary: Access to the commissary at Naval Base Guam (DeCA)
data.Exchange: Coast Guard Exchange (CGX) / access to the Navy and Air Force exchanges on Guam

intro: A Guam PCS is treated as an overseas move, with island-specific steps like vehicle shipping. Start early.

steps.title: Confirm your entitlements
steps.text: Verify OHA, COLA, DoDEA eligibility, and travel entitlements for a non-foreign OCONUS tour with finance early.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose government housing or get approval and help to rent on the economy with OHA; claim temporary lodging allowances while you look.

steps.title: Plan your vehicle and getting around
steps.text: Arrange vehicle shipping early (it takes time to the island) and handle Guam registration on arrival.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Guam schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program so you can be seen at the CG clinic and U.S. Naval Hospital Guam; some complex care requires off-island referral.

data.q: Do I get BAH on Guam?
data.a: No. Guam is non-foreign OCONUS, so you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for economy rentals (plus COLA), not the stateside BAH. That's why this guide shows OHA instead of a BAH calculator.
Guam is a U.S. territory, so it's American soil, but a tour here is treated as overseas for pay and logistics, which is why we group it under OCONUS.
In government/military housing or on the economy around the island with OHA. Guam has a large military community, so services are well established.
Tropical and beautiful, with great diving and beaches, but remote: higher prices, shipping times, and typhoon season to plan for.
